title: Hotel and accommodation managers and proprietors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that provides lodging and other accommodations. All personal service managers not listed separately. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Greet and register guests, answer inquiries pertaining to hotel policies and services, and resolve occupants' complaints.
Interview and hire applicants, including screening job applicants and recommending personnel actions such as promotions, transfers, and dismissals.
Monitor the revenue activity of the hotel or facility, including collecting payments and recording data pertaining to funds and expenditures.
Provide assistance to staff members by inspecting rooms, setting tables, or doing laundry, and performing or assisting with cleaning duties as necessary.
Book tickets for guests for local tours and attractions, and schedule guest appointments.
Organize and coordinate the work of staff and convention personnel for meetings to be held at a particular facility, and plan programs of events or schedules of activities.
Perform marketing and public relations activities, and develop or implement marketing strategies.
Arrange telephone answering services, deliver mail and packages, or answer questions regarding locations for eating and entertainment.
Purchase supplies, arrange for outside services, and manage and maintain temporary or permanent lodging facilities.
Receive and process advance registration payments, mail letters of confirmation, or return checks when registrations cannot be accepted.
Prepare required paperwork pertaining to departmental functions, and maintain required records of work hours, budgets, and payrolls.
Assign duties to workers, schedule shifts, and prepare employee work schedules.
Inspect guest rooms, public areas, and grounds for cleanliness and appearance, and check and maintain equipment to ensure that it is in working order.
Confer and cooperate with other managers to ensure coordination of hotel activities and participate in financial activities, such as the setting of room rates and establishing budgets.
Observe and monitor staff performance to ensure efficient operations and adherence to facility's policies and procedures, and train staff members in policies and procedures.
Show, rent, or assign accommodations, and explain rules and regulations of facilities to customers.
Develop and implement policies and procedures for the operation of a department or establishment and establish operational standards and procedures.
Investigate complaints about service and equipment, take corrective action, and recommend changes to improve service and increase operational efficiency.
Inspect and evaluate the physical condition of facilities to determine the type of work required, and verify all regulatory policies and procedures have been documented and communicated.
Direct activities for stopping the spread of infections in facilities, and monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health and safety standards.
Calculate and record departmental expenses and revenue, track cost-containment strategies, and prepare reports on activity, personnel, and expenses.
Contribute to the development or implementation of business strategies and maintain client databases.
Train staff in the use or sale of products, programs, or activities and provide technical support for the design, construction, or commissioning of projects.

Provide employee training on compliance-related topics, policies, or procedures, monitor compliance systems for effectiveness, identify compliance issues for follow-up, disseminate written policies related to compliance activities, file compliance reports with regulatory agencies, and maintain documentation of compliance activities.

Report and investigate violations of compliance or regulatory standards, support internal or external audits in compliance reviews, conduct internal investigations of compliance issues, and supervise audit programs related to operational, safety, or compliance standards.

Advise management on the development and implementation of compliance programs, collaborate with human resources on disciplinary actions for violations, consult with corporate attorneys on legal compliance issues, and train compliance staff on loss prevention measures.

Monitor and review facility operations, ensuring adherence to policies and safety regulations; ensure correct use of physical security systems, including surveillance equipment; and organize and oversee events or activities at hospitality and recreation facilities.

Track attendance, participation, and performance data related to wellness events, develop fitness or wellness programs and classes, conduct assessments of wellness programs for effectiveness, and provide consultations on wellness-related topics such as nutrition and fitness.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations such as workplace concerns, maintain daily operation records, and evaluate and make improvements in loss prevention programs and procedures.

Manage or oversee fitness and wellness facilities, including ensuring cleanliness and safety, coordinating health screenings or preventive measures, and serving as a contact for employee inquiries related to wellness programs.
Develop marketing campaigns to promote participation in wellness programs, advise on program offerings to enhance fitness and wellness, and respond to customer inquiries about services or programs.
Establish relationships with customers and stakeholders, manage project schedules, budgets, and deliverables, and communicate updates to project teams to ensure objectives and satisfaction are met.
Train wellness and fitness staff, supervise fitness or wellness specialists, and maintain accurate wellness- and fitness-related schedules or records.
